TextENGNew: ``Thank you so very much for reading 'Watanagashi' all the way through!@k@vS20/02/440200175. So, what did you think of this arc?@k@vS20/02/440200175_01. I'd be delighted if you enjoyed it even a little bit.``

``A little?! That's beside the point!@k@vS20/04/440400161. Not again!@k@vS20/04/440400162. It was yet another massive bad ending, was it not?!@k@vS20/04/440400163. I am absolutely furious, I'll have you know?!``
``......I'm furious too.``

``Ahh, great work out there, everyone~!!@k@vS20/03/440300305. Well, you know what?@k@vS20/03/440300306. The ending aside, wasn't this arc essentially the 'Mion Chapter'?@k@vS20/03/440300307. I played a huge role and I'm totally satisfied! Nya-ha-ha-ha!!``
changeReason: Old says 'don't you think this story was kind of like a Mion Chapter?' but new says 'wasn't this arc essentially the Mion Chapter?'; same idea but old says 'kind of like' (uncertain) vs new 'essentially' (assertive).

TextENGNew: ``Y-Y-You can't call that playing a huge role!!@k@vS20/04/440400165. Rika and I both got killed, you know?!``
changeReason: Old says 'You cannot call such a thing a success!!' but new says 'You can't call that playing a huge role!!'; 'success' vs 'playing a huge role'.

TextENGNew: ``......And Satoko was nothing more than collateral damage.``
changeReason: Old says 'None of this concerned Satoko' but ただのとばっちり means 'mere collateral damage.'

TextENGNew: ``Waaaaaaaaaaahhhhh!!!@k@vS20/04/440400167. I didn't want to die like thaaaat~!!``

``Come now, don't cry!@k@vS20/02/440200177. Just like there was a story centered on Mii-chan, there'll be one centered on Satoko-chan too, you know.@k@vS20/02/440200178. Something to look forward to, right?``
``I'm sure it'll be a story where Satoko suffers terribly, getting slowly and methodically tormented~``
changeReason: Old says 'Satoko suffers some terrible demise in some slow, deliberate way' but new says 'Satoko suffers terribly, getting slowly and methodically tormented'; 'terrible demise' (death) vs 'terribly tormented'—death vs torture.

TextENGNew: ``Waaaaahhhhh!!@k@vS20/04/440400169. I don't want thaaaat~!!!@k@vS20/04/440400170. Waaaaaaaaahhhhh!!``
``Mii~chan.@k@vS20/02/440200180. If you keep teasing like that, even Rena's going to get a little upset, you know~?``
``......Mii is flat as a board.@k@vS20/05/440500107. ...Even if you wanted to pat her head, you couldn't tell where it starts.``
changeReason: Old says 'Mii has a flat chest. Your mind wouldn't understand even if it wanted to be petted' but new says 'Mii is flat as a board. Even if you wanted to pat her head, you couldn't tell where it starts'; the joke is about flatness making it hard to find the head—new is clearer.

TextENGNew: ``Oh, they're expanding.@k@vS20/04/440400172. ...What a shameless body you have...``
``Hey there, everyone!@k@vS20/08/440800099. Great work out there!``
``Sorry we're late again. Nfu-fu-fu-fu!``
``Tomitake-san! Ooishi-san!@k@vS20/02/440200182. Thank you both for all your hard work.@k@vS20/02/440200183. Hm?@k@vS20/02/440200184. Are Takano-san and Shii-chan not here yet?``
changeReason: Old says 'Yes, you did a great job as well. Hm? Where are Takano-san and Shion-chan?' but new says 'Thank you both for all your hard work. Hm? Are Takano-san and Shii-chan not here yet?'; 'Shion-chan' vs 'Shii-chan' nickname difference.

TextENGNew: ``Of course I'm here. Thank you for having us today.@k@vS20/06/440600071. ...Mind if I sit here?``
changeReason: Old says 'They're coming' (plural) but Japanese means 'Of course I'm here' (singular first person).

TextENGNew: ``Of course, please do!``
``It's quite a sight with all of us gathered like this.@k@vS20/09/440900099. Please, go easy on me.``
changeReason: Old says 'this is sure to be the best part' but new says 'It's quite a sight with all of us gathered like this'; different observation.

TextENGNew: ``Well then, shall we get started?@k@vS20/02/440200186. Is this everyone?``
``......Everyone except Keiichi.``
``So that means... Maebara-san still hasn't finished recording and is already doing script readings for the next arc...?``
changeReason: Old says 'is as busy as ever recording the script. Has he already started recording the script for the next story...?' but new says 'still hasn't finished recording and is already doing script readings for the next arc...?'; more specific in new.

TextENGNew: ``Poor thing.@k@vS20/09/440900101. It doesn't feel right to have fun without him.``
``Takano-san, you don't need to worry about that.@k@vS20/06/440600073. At times like these, having a blast is actually the more considerate thing to do.``
``That's so true~!!@k@vS20/04/440400175. With this many of us gathered, it'd be a waste to sit around like it's a funeral~!!``
changeReason: Old says 'Holding a wake would be boring~!!' but new says 'it'd be a waste to sit around like it's a funeral~!!'; 'wake' vs 'funeral'—JPN お通夜 = wake/vigil.

TextENGNew: ``Tahahaha...@k@vS20/08/440800101. Well then... sorry, Keiichi-kun... but let's get this party started without you!``
``Then it's settled!!@k@vS20/03/440300310. Alright everyone, grab your cups!@k@vS20/03/440300311. Anyone here who doesn't like carbonated drinks~?``
changeReason: Old says 'Does anyone not like carbonated drinks~?' but new says 'Anyone here who doesn't like carbonated drinks~?'; nearly identical but old uses 'not like' vs new uses 'doesn't like'.

TextENGNew: ``And there's alcohol for the grown-ups. Nfu-fu-fu-fu!``
``What'll you have, Rika-chan?@k@vS20/08/440800103. Grape juice?@k@vS20/08/440800104. Orange juice?``
``......Mii.@k@vS20/05/440500110. I want the same fizzy barley tea that Tomitake is having.``
changeReason: Old says 'I want what Tomitake's having, barley tea' but new says 'I want the same fizzy barley tea that Tomitake is having'; new adds 'fizzy' (泡 = foam/fizz).

TextENGNew: ``Hey, now!@k@vS20/02/440200188. No alcohol for you either, Rika-chan!``
``Does everyone have their cups~?@k@vS20/04/440400177. If we don't toast soon, my juice is going to get warm~!``
``Sis, who's leading the toast?``
``Rena can do it.@k@vS20/03/440300313. Go ahead, Rena!``
``Then... ahem!@k@vS20/02/440200190. Everyone, congratulations on finishing Watanagashi~!!``
changeReason: Old says 'excellent work—the Watanagashi chapter is now over~!!' but new says 'congratulations on finishing Watanagashi~!!'; 'chapter is now over' vs 'finishing'.

TextENGNew: ``````Great work, everyone~~~!!!!``````
changeReason: Old says 'Thank you' but お疲れさまでした means 'good work/well done.'

TextENGNew: Yayyy~~~~!!! Clap clap clap clap!!!!
changeReason: Old has 'Wahhh~~~~!!!' but new has 'Yayyy~~~~!!!'; different exclamation.

TextENGNew: ``Alright, now that we're all here!@k@vS20/08/440800107. How about we discuss this arc's story again?``
changeReason: Old says 'everybody's here! Shall we debate the story again?' but new says 'now that we're all here! How about we discuss this arc's story again?'; 'debate' vs 'discuss'.

TextENGNew: ``Good idea.@k@vS20/02/440200192. Let's open the floor for opinions, then.@k@vS20/02/440200193. Does anyone have something they'd like to say~?!``
changeReason: Old says 'let's get some honest opinions. Does anyone here have something to say~?!' but new says 'Let's open the floor for opinions, then. Does anyone have something they'd like to say~?!'; stylistically similar.

TextENGNew: ``What's there to discuss? ...Weren't all the mysteries solved in this arc?@k@vS20/04/440400180. The culprit was Mion-san!@k@vS20/04/440400181. The root of aaaaall evil was the Sonozaki family!@k@vS20/04/440400182. Isn't that the end of it?``
``Hmm... I suppose so.@k@vS20/02/440200195. I really did believe... that Mii-chan wasn't the culprit, though...``
``How naive of you~!@k@vS20/06/440600077. Wasn't this entirely predictable from the previous Onikakushi arc?@k@vS20/06/440600078. If anything, it was so predictable it's almost disappointing.``
changeReason: Old says 'This was entirely predictable' adding 'In fact, it's almost shocking how predictable it was' but new says 'it was so predictable it's almost disappointing'; 'shocking' vs 'disappointing'.

TextENGNew: ``Indeed.@k@vS20/11/440700378. It likely aligns with what the majority of players predicted after the previous arc.``
``......It seems the story unfolded exactly the way everyone expected.``

``Then... does that mean... the main Higurashi When They Cry storyline is essentially over...?@k@vS20/02/440200197. So it's settled... that the source of everything was Mii-chan's Sonozaki family...?``
``Are you so sure about that?@k@vS20/09/440900104. Personally... I'd say this actually disproves the theory that the Sonozakis are behind it all.``
changeReason: Old says 'the theory of the Sonozakis being the guilty party has been disproven' but new says 'this actually disproves the theory that the Sonozakis are behind it all'; similar but 'guilty party' vs 'behind it all'.

TextENGNew: ``Oh, I share the same view as Takano-san.@k@vS20/03/440300316. I feel the same way.@k@vS20/03/440300317. ...I don't believe in curses, mind you.@k@vS20/03/440300318. But at the very least, I think the odds of the Sonozaki family being the true culprits have actually decreased.``
``Huh?@k@vS20/02/440200199. How so?@k@vS20/02/440200200. ...But Mii-chan confessed to killing everyone herself, didn't she...?``
``Exactly.@k@vS20/04/440400184. After she went and killed Rika and me, how can you say she's not the culprit after all that??``

``I'm not trying to be evasive here.@k@vS20/03/440300320. It's just that there are too many things that don't add up if Mion is supposedly behind everything.@k@vS20/03/440300321. Think about it for a moment and you'll see what I mean.``
``Oh?@k@vS20/08/440800109. I was fairly convinced the truth was somewhere in the vicinity of the Sonozaki family theory...@k@vS20/08/440800110. I'd love to hear your take, Mion-chan!``

``Then lend me your ears.@k@vS20/03/440300323. ...At the end, I confessed to being involved in all the past incidents, right?@k@vS20/03/440300324. I suppose the strong sense of something being off about that is what I'm basing this on.``

``Just think about it.@k@vS20/03/440300326. If the Sonozaki family truly committed those past crimes, that would be remarkable, wouldn't it?!@k@vS20/03/440300327. Every single one of them was practically a perfect crime, after all.@k@vS20/03/440300328. They were the ultimate offenses--each one resolved as the work of other suspects or dismissed as accidents.``
changeReason: New adds 'They were the ultimate offenses--each one resolved as the work of other suspects or dismissed as accidents' elaborating on the old's simpler 'They were all attributed to other criminals or simple accidents'.

TextENGNew: ``Well, obviously.@k@vS20/06/440600080. The Sonozaki family wields enormous power and has been operating in the shadows.@k@vS20/06/440600081. Wouldn't pulling off perfect crimes be child's play for them?``

``That's exactly it. That's what doesn't sit right!@k@vS20/03/440300330. For four straight years, the Sonozaki family killed and disappeared people so skillfully that not even a trace of suspicion fell on them, right?``

``But compared to that, the fifth year's incident was incredibly sloppy.@k@vS20/03/440300332. People were being killed and disappeared left and right, indiscriminately.@k@vS20/03/440300333. And the connection to the Sonozaki family was painfully obvious.``

``Now that you mention it, you're right.@k@vS20/02/440200202. The past incidents were all these enigmatic cases where you couldn't tell if they were the curse or criminal acts.@k@vS20/02/440200203. ...But the fifth year's incident was suddenly so crude and violent.``
``I haven't the faintest idea what you're all going on about.@k@vS20/04/440400186. ...Rika, do you understand?``
``......What she's saying is that if the Sonozaki family were truly the culprits, they would have handled the fifth year far more skillfully.``

``Then... what does that mean?@k@vS20/04/440400188. Mion-san didn't kill Rika and me--it was the work of a demon that possessed her?@k@|@y@vS20/04/440400189. See, see!!@k@vS20/04/440400190. Just as I predicted last time, Mion-san wasn't a bad person after all~!!``
changeReason: Old says 'Just like I thought last time, Mion-san wasn't a bad person after all, was she~?!' but new says 'Just as I predicted last time, Mion-san wasn't a bad person after all~!!'; 'thought' vs 'predicted'.

TextENGNew: ``Exactly.@k@vS20/02/440200205. Both Satoko-chan and I thought that she was doing terrible things because something had possessed her.@k@vS20/02/440200206. So... does that mean our theory was right all along?!``
``I've maintained the curse theory since the previous afterparty, and this arc has only strengthened my conviction.``
``......That revelation at the very end,@k@vS20/11/440700381. the fact that Takano-san and Mion-san had been dead for days despite being seen alive--that seems to strongly support the curse theory.``
changeReason: Old says 'the information regarding how Mion-san and Takano-san had died days before, despite being witnessed, seems to indeed support that theory' but new has nearly identical meaning with clearer structure.

TextENGNew: ``My, my...@k@vS20/09/440900106. The curse faction, which seemed so tentative last time, has grown rather confident.@k@vS20/09/440900107. Don't any of you who believe it was a human culprit have objections?``
``Last time, the ones who thought it was a human were Mii-chan and Rika-chan.@k@vS20/02/440200208. And Tomitake-san too, right?@k@vS20/02/440200209. Alright then, let's hear your counterarguments!``
``Hmm, you've got me stumped.@k@vS20/08/440800112. I just can't find the right words...``
``One of the pillars of the curse theory is the discrepancy in time of death, right?@k@vS20/06/440600083. So if we could debunk that, it would prove the culprit was human, wouldn't it?``
``Oh? Shion, do you have a good counterargument?``

``Well, sort of.@k@vS20/06/440600085. I agree with you, Sis, that it was a human... but I differ on whether the Sonozaki family were the masterminds.@k@vS20/06/440600086. If that's alright, I'll present my objection.``
changeReason: Old says 'I don't think the Sonozaki family were the main culprits either' but new says 'I differ on whether the Sonozaki family were the masterminds'; 'main culprits' vs 'masterminds'.

TextENGNew: ``Oho-ho-ho-ho!@k@vS20/04/440400192. By all means!@k@vS20/04/440400193. If you claim this wasn't the curse, then show us your proof~!!``

``Well then, Shion-chan.@k@vS20/09/440900109. That trick at the end we've all been discussing.@k@vS20/09/440900110. Despite Mion-chan having died falling into the well on the day of the incident, how did she appear days later to kill you and Keiichi-kun?``
``Simple: my theory is that the Sonozaki sisters were accomplices.@k@vS20/06/440600088. The reasoning is straightforward.``

``If Mion was already dead by that point, then the only one who could have stabbed Kei-chan was me. I disguised myself as Mion and stabbed him!@k@vS20/06/440600090. Then I returned to my room, staged a struggle with a ghost, and threw myself off the balcony.``
changeReason: Old says 'pretended to have a tussle with a ghost, then jumped right down and killed myself' but new says 'staged a struggle with a ghost, and threw myself off the balcony'; 'jumped right down' vs 'threw myself off the balcony'—more specific in new.

TextENGNew: ``If I had to put it plainly, Mion-chan was the scapegoat in this case.@k@vS20/08/440800114. She would handle the village chief and the others directly, while Shion-chan would be made to look like nothing more than a victim.``
changeReason: Old says 'She would take care of the mayor in person' but new says 'She would handle the village chief and the others directly'; 'mayor' vs 'village chief' for 村長.

TextENGNew: ``And by hiding herself in the well, the disguised Shion-chan could appear and disappear at will, committing murders wherever she pleased--all according to plan.``

``I see.@k@vS20/11/440700383. ...In that case, Mion-san made two miscalculations.@k@vS20/11/440700384. The first was her body being discovered, and the second was actually dying when she'd only meant to hide.@k@vS20/11/440700385. That's what it would come down to.``
``...Oh my, well now...@k@vS20/02/440200211. ...You're right... that does seem to hold together somehow...``

``But... it's still not convincing, is it!@k@vS20/03/440300336. It's too different from the elegance of the past perfect crimes.@k@vS20/03/440300337. ...Even if the plan succeeded, 'Mion' would end up the villain, wouldn't she?@k@vS20/03/440300338. She'd have to spend the rest of her life in hiding, or live pretending to be Shion, wouldn't she?``
changeReason: Old says 'its purpose was to cement all the past incidents as perfect crimes' but new says 'it was the final act designed to cement all the past incidents as perfect crimes'; adds 'final act' framing.

TextENGNew: ``That's far too suffocating!@k@vS20/03/440300340. You can't call something like that a perfect crime!``
``......In other words, whether through death or disappearance, Mii would have to vanish entirely.``

``Rika-chama, that's a great insight.@k@vS20/06/440600092. In other words, Mion Sonozaki leaving the stage was part of their plan all along.@k@vS20/06/440600093. That's why, at the very end, Mion so readily took the blame for all the past incidents.@k@vS20/06/440600094. She took full responsibility onto herself and made her exit!``
changeReason: Old says 'So while I was reading the script for the next game all by myself, you were all talking about me behind my back, huh?' but new says 'while I was slaving away reading the script for the next arc all by myself, you were all having this lively discussion about me'; 'game' vs 'arc'.

TextENGNew: ``By doing so, the true culprit behind the past incidents stays hidden.@k@vS20/03/440300342. ...Meaning this year's incident wasn't sloppy at all--it was the final act designed to cement all the past incidents as perfect crimes.@k@vS20/03/440300343. ...Is that how it fits together...?``

``The young head of the Sonozaki family--the prime suspect--confesses to everything and vanishes.@k@vS20/09/440900112. ......For her, or perhaps for whoever the non-Sonozaki true culprit might be, there could be no more convenient outcome.``
``............I don't understand any of this conversation at all.@k@vS20/04/440400195. What do you mean, Mion-san is 'taking the blame'?@k@vS20/04/440400196. Isn't she just... the culprit...??``

``Let me explain.@k@vS20/02/440200213. The true culprit behind the serial mysterious deaths is someone else entirely.@k@vS20/02/440200214. To protect that person, Mii-chan made a false confession claiming she was behind all the past incidents... that's what everyone's suggesting.``
``Well, well... to borrow a line from the story... it seems we have quite the brilliant detective among us!@k@vS20/11/440700387. I must admit... that's rather persuasive!``

``An incident orchestrated to throw investigators off the true culprit's trail.@k@vS20/03/440300345. That's my theory.@k@vS20/03/440300346. And I'll go a step further--I believe the true culprit is someone outside the Sonozaki family entirely.``
``So you're saying Mion-chan created a diversionary incident to further protect the mysterious figure who pulled off four consecutive years of perfect crimes?``
``Exactly.@k@vS20/03/440300348. That 'Director' who was only mentioned by name in the Onikakushi arc is looking awfully suspicious, if you ask me!``
changeReason: Old says 'The director who only appeared by name' but new says 'That Director who was only mentioned by name in the Onikakushi arc'; new adds 'in the Onikakushi arc' specificity.

TextENGNew: ``......Impressive.@k@vS20/05/440500116. It really does all add up.``
Clap clap clap.

``I still believe the Sonozaki family and the sisters are the culprits, though.@k@vS20/06/440600096. An incident the sisters carried out to sever the old bonds tying them to the Three Families--to settle accounts with the past.@k@vS20/06/440600097. That's my theory.``

``Settling accounts with the past...@k@vS20/08/440800117. You mean Mion, as the final head of the family, took the blame for all the Three Families' sins to bring their dark history to a close--and then the two sisters bowed out together.``
changeReason: Old says 'Two Families' but should reference 御三家 (Three Families).

TextENGNew: ``Precisely.@k@vS20/06/440600099. A tragic tale of two sisters who agonized endlessly before finally choosing this path.@k@|@y@vS20/06/440600100. Haha, that's almost too neat, isn't it!``
``......Impressive.@k@vS20/05/440500118. It really does all add up.``
Clap clap clap.
``Hm, what's this?@k@vS20/02/440200216. It looks like Rika-chan has something to say too.@k@vS20/02/440200217. Go ahead, Rika-chan!``
``......Then I have a question for everyone.@k@vS20/05/440500120. ...Why did Keiichi have to be stabbed?``
``...That's obvious, Rika.@k@vS20/04/440400198. It's because he went and entered the forbidden ritual storehouse without permission.``
``Come to think of it, Tomitake-san and Takano-san must have died in the other arc because they snuck into the storehouse too, right?@k@vS20/11/440700389. ............Huh?``
changeReason: Old says 'Thinking back on it now, Tomitake-kun and Takano-san probably died in the previous story because they snuck into that storehouse' but new says 'in the other arc' (plural implication); also 'previous story' vs 'other arc'.

TextENGNew: ``......*giggle*@k@vS20/09/440900115. Things are getting rather curious now, aren't they?``
``Huh?@k@vS20/02/440200219. What do you mean, curious...?``
``...Ah!!@k@vS20/04/440400200. Rena-san, that's it!@k@vS20/04/440400201. Think back, if you would?!``
``......Exactly.@k@vS20/05/440500122. ...In the Onikakushi arc, for example, Keiichi was targeted even though he never set foot in the ritual storehouse.``
changeReason: Old says 'they were after Keiichi's life even though he didn't sneak into the ritual storehouse' but new says 'Keiichi was targeted even though he never set foot in the ritual storehouse'; 'after his life' vs 'targeted'.

TextENGNew: ``That must be because... right!@k@vS20/08/440800119. Hinamizawa despises outsiders, doesn't it?@k@vS20/08/440800120. That's why they went after Keiichi-kun...!``

``That doesn't hold up, though.@k@vS20/03/440300350. ...In that case, Kei-chan's parents should have been targeted too.@k@vS20/03/440300351. But no matter how you look at it, his parents never seemed to be in any danger.@k@vS20/03/440300352. Not just this time--in the other arcs either.``
``Well now, those of you in the human culprit camp~?!@k@vS20/04/440400203. Unless you can properly explain why Keiichi-san was targeted, you can't prove the culprit was human, can you~?!?!``
``Nah-ha-ha-ha.@k@vS20/11/440700391. But even with the curse theory, we still can't explain why Maebara-san was attacked in both this arc and the last.``
``We were so close to figuring it out, but...@k@vS20/02/440200221. it seems there are still plenty of mysteries left unsolved...``

``*giggle*......@k@vS20/09/440900117. We can find some rationale for everyone else's deaths... but Keiichi-kun's is always strangely vague.@k@vS20/09/440900118. ...In Onikakushi, in Taraimawashi, and now in Watanagashi.@k@vS20/09/440900119. He was targeted regardless of whether he entered the ritual storehouse.``
changeReason: Old says 'Last time in Onikakushi and Taraimawashi. And now in Watanagashi.' but new says 'In Onikakushi, in Taraimawashi, and now in Watanagashi.'; also old says 'regardless of whether he went into the ritual storehouse' while JPN mentions this is unrelated—similar.

TextENGNew: ``......Then perhaps he did something in the other arcs, too--something that warranted being killed...?``
``...I see...@k@vS20/11/440700393. If we could find a common thread in Maebara-san's actions across arcs... that could be the key to unraveling the entire mystery...``

``I skimmed through the scripts side by side, but... I think Keiichi-kun was just living his life normally.@k@vS20/08/440800122. ...Aside from the storehouse incident, I can't find anything suspicious about his everyday routine!``
changeReason: Old says 'I skimmed through the stories again' but new says 'I skimmed through the scripts side by side'; 'stories' vs 'scripts side by side'—new implies comparison.

TextENGNew: ``Hmm...@k@vS20/02/440200223. ...Keiichi-kun... what did you do across all these arcs...?@k@vS20/02/440200224. Something bad enough to get yourself killed!``
``......When you're at a loss, the best thing to do is ask the person himself.``

``......Hohoh.@k@vS20/01/440100203. ...So while I was slaving away reading the script for the next arc all by myself, you were all having this lively discussion about me.@k@vS20/01/440100204. I see...``
``Well, that's the gist of it.@k@vS20/02/440200226. So, Keiichi-kun, we'd like you to search your own heart and tell us...``
``Kei-chan, what terrible thing did you do that would get you killed?``
``It must be because of all the terrible things he does to me on a daily basis~!!``
changeReason: Old says 'all the violent things he does towards me' but new says 'all the terrible things he does to me on a daily basis'; 'violent' vs 'terrible'—different characterization.

TextENGNew: ``The way he sometimes turns that dangerous gaze on me and gets lost in his fantasies--he looks like a genuine criminal.``
changeReason: Old says 'makes strange deluded expressions is surely a criminal act' but new says 'gets lost in his fantasies—he looks like a genuine criminal'; 'deluded expressions' vs 'lost in fantasies'.

TextENGNew: ``Right, right!@k@vS20/08/440800124. Being surrounded by girls his age and living happily every day--that's basically a crime in itself, wouldn't you say?``

``Nah-ha-ha-ha-ha!!@k@vS20/11/440700395. A grave crime indeed!@k@vS20/11/440700396. Maebara-san truly is an enviable protagonist, isn't he?@k@vS20/11/440700397. Let me take over the role once in a while!``
``Ku-ku-ku...!@k@vS20/09/440900121. Well, with all those perks he enjoys, getting stabbed at the end is really a small price to pay, isn't it?``
``So there you have it, Keiichi-san.@k@vS20/04/440400207. Have you recalled the sins you've committed~?``

``Mrrrgaaahhh!!!!@k@vS20/01/440100206. This time and every other arc!!@k@vS20/01/440100207. I live a clean, upstanding life, yet I always end up suffering at the end!!@k@vS20/01/440100208. And now you want me to 'search my own heart'?!@k@vS20/01/440100209. I'm the one who wants answers here! This is a conspiracy!!!!``
changeReason: Old says 'I live a proper and just life, and I always meet a terrible fate in the end!! What was that about ask my own heart? I'm the one who wants to know! This is bullying!!!!' but new says 'I live a clean, upstanding life, yet I always end up suffering at the end!! ...I'm the one who wants answers here! This is a conspiracy!!!!'; 'bullying' vs 'conspiracy' JPN 謀略 = conspiracy.

TextENGNew: ``.........Poor thing, poor thing.@k@vS20/05/440500126. I'll pat your head for you next time.``
``I wonder if we ought to go back and reread Onikakushi now that we've read this one...?``
``If you're going to say that, I have a feeling there are mysteries hidden in the other arcs as well.``
``I'm going to reread Watanagashi from Mion's perspective.@k@vS20/03/440300355. I might discover something different from when I read it through Kei-chan's eyes.``
``I will always believe it was the work of a 'demon'!!@k@vS20/04/440400209. Every last event and trick can be explained by a 'demon'!!!``
changeReason: Old says 'All of the events and tricks can be explained with a demon!!!' but new says 'Every last event and trick can be explained by a demon!!!'; similar but old uses 'demon' while JPN uses 鬼 which the old already had in quotes.

TextENGNew: ``......That sounds rather like the plasma argument.``
``What did all of you who played think about it?@k@vS20/02/440200228. By all means, try coming up with your own theories before moving on to the next arc!``
``There's still a long road ahead, so hang in there~!``
``With that, please continue to enjoy the story.``
